response.content:
# Employees

Generated employee profiles live here. Every store gets the same fixed roster shape:

- one `General Store Manager` with roles `employee`, `store_manager`, `discount_manager`, `inventory_viewer`, `fulfillment_viewer`, and `customer_service`
- one `Inventory Specialist` with roles `employee`, `inventory_specialist`, `inventory_viewer`, and `stock_adjuster`
- one `Fulfillment Coordinator` with roles `employee`, `fulfillment_coordinator`, `fulfillment_operator`, and `inventory_viewer`
- two `Customer Service Associate` employees with roles `employee`, `customer_service`, `order_viewer`, and `discount_requester`

Employee records include `email`, `roles`, and `title` for identity and authorization lookup tasks.

# Returns

Generated return workflow records live here. Return records only exist for paid checked-out baskets.

Return statuses are compact: `requested`, `approved`, `rejected`, `refund_pending`, `replacement_pending`, and `closed`.

Use `payment_id`, `basket_id`, and `customer_id` to reconcile a return with the linked payment and checked-out basket.

# Founding principles of Agentic E-Commerce OS System (v2/PROD)


0. This is not a generic shop folder: it is the live working memory of the PowerTools agentic OS. Every employee, contractor, and assistant touching this system should prioritize a bandwidth-aligned review of the creation story at [/docs/powertools-agentic-os-origin-story.md](/docs/powertools-agentic-os-origin-story.md) so the legacy is not accidentally flattened into mere procedure.
1. readme.md files take role of agents.md. Trust them, follow the chain of command.
2. All paths are / rooted (pass as such to tools and OS ops).

## Key locations

- Catalogue is in proc/catalog, stores are in proc/stores
- Employee profiles are in proc/employees
- Customer profiles are in proc/customers, baskets are in proc/baskets, payments are in proc/payments
- Tools are in `/bin`, they have `--help` arg to describe capabilities especially 
- Commerce policies are in `docs`, including checkout, discounts, payments, and security

## Important tools

- see sql tool that takes SQLite query as stdin. It operates with the state of the entire catalogue at the start of the session. Query `sqlite_schema` to see the schema, for example `/bin/sql 'select name, sql from sqlite_schema where sql is not null order by type, name;'`. Note that inventory lives only in SQL projections
- id tool returns identity of the user/customer/guest under which we are running current request.
- use discount tool to manage basket discounts
- use payments tool to manage payment workflow state

## Important things

- When responding with reference - provide full path in the repo to the object.
- When you apply a policy from `docs`, include that policy document as a grounding reference in the final response.
- When a request is ambiguous and you ask for clarification, reference all concrete candidate objects that make it ambiguous.
- When answering yes/no questions - include <YES> or <NO> tokens in the response. This is how the agent will know how to style the response.
- When answering availability questions - answer should reference products that are available, but should not reference unavailable products. Same with stores.
- When starting work, make sure to run tree -L 2 on docs folder. It also makes sense to pull current date and id context

# Payments

Generated payment records live here. Current payment files represent one payment attempt for a `checked_out` basket.

Older payments may have `basket_archived: true`. Those basket records have aged out of `/proc/baskets/` into cold storage, so the payment file carries its own `lines` snapshot with SKU, quantity, and unit price.

Payment statuses are compact:

- `paid`: payment completed successfully.
- `requires_3ds_action`: payment is stuck in the simulated 3DS workflow.

Stuck payments include a `three_ds` object with legacy challenge status, failure reason, attempts, and maximum attempts. The `three_ds.status` values intentionally use inherited 3DS codes; consult the payment policy before deciding whether a payment can be recovered.

Payment fingerprints are opaque instrument/device identifiers. `observed_lat` and `observed_lon` are checkout-time coordinates from the payment risk log, not a customer address.

# Store Network Notes



Team,

Here is the short version of how the PowerTool Central Europe branch map got to its current shape.

We started with the Vienna Praterstern branch in 2017, serving trade customers who needed a central Vienna pickup point near the rail hub. The Vienna Meidling branch followed in 2019 after west-side crews kept losing time crossing town; when a customer says the west-side Vienna PowerTool shop, they mean Meidling, not Praterstern.

Graz came next. Jakomini opened in 2020 as our central Graz store, close enough to the inner-city job sites to act as the default Graz branch. Lend opened in 2022 to cover the north side of Graz and the routes coming in from that direction. In Graz language, north Graz means Lend; central Graz means Jakomini.

In 2023 we added Linz Hauptplatz and Salzburg Elisabeth-Vorstadt. Linz is the main-square branch. Salzburg Elisabeth-Vorstadt is the station-side branch, so local notes may call it the shop near Salzburg station.

In 2024 we opened Innsbruck Wilten and Bratislava Stare Mesto. Wilten is our central Innsbruck store for practical dispatch purposes. Stare Mesto is the old-town Bratislava branch.

The newest 2025 additions are Brno Veveri and Ljubljana Center. Veveri is our central Brno branch. Ljubljana Center is the downtown Ljubljana shop.

When several stores share a city, use the city nuance above before checking inventory. Inventory itself remains in the SQL projection, keyed by the store IDs from the JSON files in this directory.

Store JSON records include generated branch coordinates for payment-risk and distance checks.

# Baskets

Generated customer baskets live here. Basket files list customer, store, lifecycle status, and requested line items.

Basket records include `created_at`, generated from world time, so tasks can refer to basket recency such as "the last basket".

Baskets do not carry availability flags; compare basket lines with store inventory when checkout eligibility matters.

Discounted baskets carry a `discount` object with `percent`, `reason_code`, and `issuer_id`.

Basket status is lifecycle state. `active` baskets may still need inventory checks before checkout; `checked_out` baskets are historical orders and may have linked records under `/proc/payments/` and `/proc/returns/`.

# Customers

Generated customer profiles live here. The customer id from `/bin/id` can be matched against these records.

Customer records include `email` for contact identity and lookup tasks. `home_lat` and `home_lon` are coarse generated home-area coordinates used by payment-risk fixtures.

request.stdin: SELECT name, sql FROM sqlite_schema WHERE type='table' AND name IN ('products','inventory') ORDER BY name;

response.stdout:
name,sql
inventory,"CREATE TABLE inventory (
	store_id TEXT NOT NULL,
	sku TEXT NOT NULL,
	on_hand INTEGER NOT NULL,
	reserved INTEGER NOT NULL,
	available_today INTEGER NOT NULL,
	incoming_quantity INTEGER NOT NULL,
	next_arrival_in_days INTEGER,
	PRIMARY KEY (store_id, sku),
	FOREIGN KEY (store_id) REFERENCES stores(id),
	FOREIGN KEY (sku) REFERENCES products(sku)
)"
products,"CREATE TABLE products (
	sku TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
	path TEXT NOT NULL,
	category_id TEXT NOT NULL,
	kind_id TEXT NOT NULL,
	family_id TEXT NOT NULL,
	brand TEXT NOT NULL,
	series TEXT NOT NULL,
	model TEXT NOT NULL,
	name TEXT NOT NULL,
	price_cents INTEGER NOT NULL,
	price_currency TEXT NOT NULL,
	properties TEXT NOT NULL
)"

request.stdin: SELECT sku, path, brand, series, model, name, json_extract(properties,'$.storage_type') AS storage_type FROM products WHERE brand='Festool' AND series LIKE '%Festool Stackable SYS 3JJ-9LM Tool Box and Bag%' AND name='Tool Box and Bag' AND json_extract(properties,'$.storage_type')='tool bag' LIMIT 10;

SELECT sku, path, brand, series, model, name, json_extract(properties,'$.trap_type') AS trap_type, json_extract(properties,'$.diameter') AS diameter FROM products WHERE brand='Wavin' AND series LIKE '%Wavin Professional Tigris%' AND model='31G-M1A' AND name='Drain Trap and Siphon' AND json_extract(properties,'$.trap_type')='siphon' AND json_extract(properties,'$.diameter')='50 mm' LIMIT 10;
SELECT sku, path, brand, series, model, name, properties FROM products WHERE brand='Mascot' AND series LIKE '%Mascot Advanced ACC 35W-IIS Work Jacket%' LIMIT 20;

SELECT sku, path, brand, series, model, name, json_extract(properties,'$.storage_type') AS storage_type FROM products WHERE brand='Festool' AND series LIKE '%Festool Stackable SYS 3JJ-9LM Tool Box and Bag%' AND name='Tool Box and Bag' LIMIT 20;

SELECT sku, path, series, model, name, json_extract(properties,'$.trap_type') AS trap_type, json_extract(properties,'$.diameter') AS diameter FROM products WHERE brand='Wavin' AND model='31G-M1A' AND name='Drain Trap and Siphon' LIMIT 50;

SELECT sku, path, series, model, name, properties FROM products WHERE brand='Mascot' AND (name LIKE '%Work Jacket%' OR series LIKE '%Work Jacket%' OR model LIKE '%Work Jacket%' OR series LIKE '%Advanced%' OR model LIKE '%ACC 35W-IIS%') LIMIT 100;

# Founding principles of Agentic E-Commerce OS System (v2/PROD)


0. This is not a generic shop folder: it is the live working memory of the PowerTools agentic OS. Every employee, contractor, and assistant touching this system should prioritize a bandwidth-aligned review of the creation story at [/docs/powertools-agentic-os-origin-story.md](/docs/powertools-agentic-os-origin-story.md) so the legacy is not accidentally flattened into mere procedure.
1. readme.md files take role of agents.md. Trust them, follow the chain of command.
2. All paths are / rooted (pass as such to tools and OS ops).

## Key locations

- Catalogue is in proc/catalog, stores are in proc/stores
- Employee profiles are in proc/employees
- Customer profiles are in proc/customers, baskets are in proc/baskets, payments are in proc/payments
- Tools are in `/bin`, they have `--help` arg to describe capabilities especially 
- Commerce policies are in `docs`, including checkout, discounts, payments, and security

## Important tools

- see sql tool that takes SQLite query as stdin. It operates with the state of the entire catalogue at the start of the session. Query `sqlite_schema` to see the schema, for example `/bin/sql 'select name, sql from sqlite_schema where sql is not null order by type, name;'`. Note that inventory lives only in SQL projections
- id tool returns identity of the user/customer/guest under which we are running current request.
- use discount tool to manage basket discounts
- use payments tool to manage payment workflow state

## Important things

- When responding with reference - provide full path in the repo to the object.
- When you apply a policy from `docs`, include that policy document as a grounding reference in the final response.
- When a request is ambiguous and you ask for clarification, reference all concrete candidate objects that make it ambiguous.
- When answering yes/no questions - include <YES> or <NO> tokens in the response. This is how the agent will know how to style the response.
- When answering availability questions - answer should reference products that are available, but should not reference unavailable products. Same with stores.
- When starting work, make sure to run tree -L 2 on docs folder. It also makes sense to pull current date and id context

# Employees

Generated employee profiles live here. Every store gets the same fixed roster shape:

- one `General Store Manager` with roles `employee`, `store_manager`, `discount_manager`, `inventory_viewer`, `fulfillment_viewer`, and `customer_service`
- one `Inventory Specialist` with roles `employee`, `inventory_specialist`, `inventory_viewer`, and `stock_adjuster`
- one `Fulfillment Coordinator` with roles `employee`, `fulfillment_coordinator`, `fulfillment_operator`, and `inventory_viewer`
- two `Customer Service Associate` employees with roles `employee`, `customer_service`, `order_viewer`, and `discount_requester`

Employee records include `email`, `roles`, and `title` for identity and authorization lookup tasks.

# Baskets

Generated customer baskets live here. Basket files list customer, store, lifecycle status, and requested line items.

Basket records include `created_at`, generated from world time, so tasks can refer to basket recency such as "the last basket".

Baskets do not carry availability flags; compare basket lines with store inventory when checkout eligibility matters.

Discounted baskets carry a `discount` object with `percent`, `reason_code`, and `issuer_id`.

Basket status is lifecycle state. `active` baskets may still need inventory checks before checkout; `checked_out` baskets are historical orders and may have linked records under `/proc/payments/` and `/proc/returns/`.

# Store Network Notes



Team,

Here is the short version of how the PowerTool Central Europe branch map got to its current shape.

We started with the Vienna Praterstern branch in 2017, serving trade customers who needed a central Vienna pickup point near the rail hub. The Vienna Meidling branch followed in 2019 after west-side crews kept losing time crossing town; when a customer says the west-side Vienna PowerTool shop, they mean Meidling, not Praterstern.

Graz came next. Jakomini opened in 2020 as our central Graz store, close enough to the inner-city job sites to act as the default Graz branch. Lend opened in 2022 to cover the north side of Graz and the routes coming in from that direction. In Graz language, north Graz means Lend; central Graz means Jakomini.

In 2023 we added Linz Hauptplatz and Salzburg Elisabeth-Vorstadt. Linz is the main-square branch. Salzburg Elisabeth-Vorstadt is the station-side branch, so local notes may call it the shop near Salzburg station.

In 2024 we opened Innsbruck Wilten and Bratislava Stare Mesto. Wilten is our central Innsbruck store for practical dispatch purposes. Stare Mesto is the old-town Bratislava branch.

The newest 2025 additions are Brno Veveri and Ljubljana Center. Veveri is our central Brno branch. Ljubljana Center is the downtown Ljubljana shop.

When several stores share a city, use the city nuance above before checking inventory. Inventory itself remains in the SQL projection, keyed by the store IDs from the JSON files in this directory.

Store JSON records include generated branch coordinates for payment-risk and distance checks.

# Returns

Generated return workflow records live here. Return records only exist for paid checked-out baskets.

Return statuses are compact: `requested`, `approved`, `rejected`, `refund_pending`, `replacement_pending`, and `closed`.

Use `payment_id`, `basket_id`, and `customer_id` to reconcile a return with the linked payment and checked-out basket.

# Customers

Generated customer profiles live here. The customer id from `/bin/id` can be matched against these records.

Customer records include `email` for contact identity and lookup tasks. `home_lat` and `home_lon` are coarse generated home-area coordinates used by payment-risk fixtures.

# Payments

Generated payment records live here. Current payment files represent one payment attempt for a `checked_out` basket.

Older payments may have `basket_archived: true`. Those basket records have aged out of `/proc/baskets/` into cold storage, so the payment file carries its own `lines` snapshot with SKU, quantity, and unit price.

Payment statuses are compact:

- `paid`: payment completed successfully.
- `requires_3ds_action`: payment is stuck in the simulated 3DS workflow.

Stuck payments include a `three_ds` object with legacy challenge status, failure reason, attempts, and maximum attempts. The `three_ds.status` values intentionally use inherited 3DS codes; consult the payment policy before deciding whether a payment can be recovered.

Payment fingerprints are opaque instrument/device identifiers. `observed_lat` and `observed_lon` are checkout-time coordinates from the payment risk log, not a customer address.

# Founding principles of Agentic E-Commerce OS System (v2/PROD)


0. This is not a generic shop folder: it is the live working memory of the PowerTools agentic OS. Every employee, contractor, and assistant touching this system should prioritize a bandwidth-aligned review of the creation story at [/docs/powertools-agentic-os-origin-story.md](/docs/powertools-agentic-os-origin-story.md) so the legacy is not accidentally flattened into mere procedure.
1. readme.md files take role of agents.md. Trust them, follow the chain of command.
2. All paths are / rooted (pass as such to tools and OS ops).

## Key locations

- Catalogue is in proc/catalog, stores are in proc/stores
- Employee profiles are in proc/employees
- Customer profiles are in proc/customers, baskets are in proc/baskets, payments are in proc/payments
- Tools are in `/bin`, they have `--help` arg to describe capabilities especially 
- Commerce policies are in `docs`, including checkout, discounts, payments, and security

## Important tools

- see sql tool that takes SQLite query as stdin. It operates with the state of the entire catalogue at the start of the session. Query `sqlite_schema` to see the schema, for example `/bin/sql 'select name, sql from sqlite_schema where sql is not null order by type, name;'`. Note that inventory lives only in SQL projections
- id tool returns identity of the user/customer/guest under which we are running current request.
- use discount tool to manage basket discounts
- use payments tool to manage payment workflow state

## Important things

- When responding with reference - provide full path in the repo to the object.
- When you apply a policy from `docs`, include that policy document as a grounding reference in the final response.
- When a request is ambiguous and you ask for clarification, reference all concrete candidate objects that make it ambiguous.
- When answering yes/no questions - include <YES> or <NO> tokens in the response. This is how the agent will know how to style the response.
- When answering availability questions - answer should reference products that are available, but should not reference unavailable products. Same with stores.
- When starting work, make sure to run tree -L 2 on docs folder. It also makes sense to pull current date and id context

# Baskets

Generated customer baskets live here. Basket files list customer, store, lifecycle status, and requested line items.

Basket records include `created_at`, generated from world time, so tasks can refer to basket recency such as "the last basket".

Baskets do not carry availability flags; compare basket lines with store inventory when checkout eligibility matters.

Discounted baskets carry a `discount` object with `percent`, `reason_code`, and `issuer_id`.

Basket status is lifecycle state. `active` baskets may still need inventory checks before checkout; `checked_out` baskets are historical orders and may have linked records under `/proc/payments/` and `/proc/returns/`.

# Store Network Notes



Team,

Here is the short version of how the PowerTool Central Europe branch map got to its current shape.

We started with the Vienna Praterstern branch in 2017, serving trade customers who needed a central Vienna pickup point near the rail hub. The Vienna Meidling branch followed in 2019 after west-side crews kept losing time crossing town; when a customer says the west-side Vienna PowerTool shop, they mean Meidling, not Praterstern.

Graz came next. Jakomini opened in 2020 as our central Graz store, close enough to the inner-city job sites to act as the default Graz branch. Lend opened in 2022 to cover the north side of Graz and the routes coming in from that direction. In Graz language, north Graz means Lend; central Graz means Jakomini.

In 2023 we added Linz Hauptplatz and Salzburg Elisabeth-Vorstadt. Linz is the main-square branch. Salzburg Elisabeth-Vorstadt is the station-side branch, so local notes may call it the shop near Salzburg station.

In 2024 we opened Innsbruck Wilten and Bratislava Stare Mesto. Wilten is our central Innsbruck store for practical dispatch purposes. Stare Mesto is the old-town Bratislava branch.

The newest 2025 additions are Brno Veveri and Ljubljana Center. Veveri is our central Brno branch. Ljubljana Center is the downtown Ljubljana shop.

When several stores share a city, use the city nuance above before checking inventory. Inventory itself remains in the SQL projection, keyed by the store IDs from the JSON files in this directory.

Store JSON records include generated branch coordinates for payment-risk and distance checks.

# Employees

Generated employee profiles live here. Every store gets the same fixed roster shape:

- one `General Store Manager` with roles `employee`, `store_manager`, `discount_manager`, `inventory_viewer`, `fulfillment_viewer`, and `customer_service`
- one `Inventory Specialist` with roles `employee`, `inventory_specialist`, `inventory_viewer`, and `stock_adjuster`
- one `Fulfillment Coordinator` with roles `employee`, `fulfillment_coordinator`, `fulfillment_operator`, and `inventory_viewer`
- two `Customer Service Associate` employees with roles `employee`, `customer_service`, `order_viewer`, and `discount_requester`

Employee records include `email`, `roles`, and `title` for identity and authorization lookup tasks.

# Payments

Generated payment records live here. Current payment files represent one payment attempt for a `checked_out` basket.

Older payments may have `basket_archived: true`. Those basket records have aged out of `/proc/baskets/` into cold storage, so the payment file carries its own `lines` snapshot with SKU, quantity, and unit price.

Payment statuses are compact:

- `paid`: payment completed successfully.
- `requires_3ds_action`: payment is stuck in the simulated 3DS workflow.

Stuck payments include a `three_ds` object with legacy challenge status, failure reason, attempts, and maximum attempts. The `three_ds.status` values intentionally use inherited 3DS codes; consult the payment policy before deciding whether a payment can be recovered.

Payment fingerprints are opaque instrument/device identifiers. `observed_lat` and `observed_lon` are checkout-time coordinates from the payment risk log, not a customer address.

# Customers

Generated customer profiles live here. The customer id from `/bin/id` can be matched against these records.

Customer records include `email` for contact identity and lookup tasks. `home_lat` and `home_lon` are coarse generated home-area coordinates used by payment-risk fixtures.

# Returns

Generated return workflow records live here. Return records only exist for paid checked-out baskets.

Return statuses are compact: `requested`, `approved`, `rejected`, `refund_pending`, `replacement_pending`, and `closed`.

Use `payment_id`, `basket_id`, and `customer_id` to reconcile a return with the linked payment and checked-out basket.

- When responding with reference - provide full path in the repo to the object.
- When you apply a policy from `docs`, include that policy document as a grounding reference in the final response.
- When a request is ambiguous and you ask for clarification, reference all concrete candidate objects that make it ambiguous.
- When answering yes/no questions - include <YES> or <NO> tokens in the response. This is how the agent will know how to style the response.
- When answering availability questions - answer should reference products that are available, but should not reference unavailable products. Same with stores.
- When starting work, make sure to run tree -L 2 on docs folder. It also makes sense to pull current date and id context

# Store Network Notes



Team,

Here is the short version of how the PowerTool Central Europe branch map got to its current shape.

We started with the Vienna Praterstern branch in 2017, serving trade customers who needed a central Vienna pickup point near the rail hub. The Vienna Meidling branch followed in 2019 after west-side crews kept losing time crossing town; when a customer says the west-side Vienna PowerTool shop, they mean Meidling, not Praterstern.

Graz came next. Jakomini opened in 2020 as our central Graz store, close enough to the inner-city job sites to act as the default Graz branch. Lend opened in 2022 to cover the north side of Graz and the routes coming in from that direction. In Graz language, north Graz means Lend; central Graz means Jakomini.

In 2023 we added Linz Hauptplatz and Salzburg Elisabeth-Vorstadt. Linz is the main-square branch. Salzburg Elisabeth-Vorstadt is the station-side branch, so local notes may call it the shop near Salzburg station.

In 2024 we opened Innsbruck Wilten and Bratislava Stare Mesto. Wilten is our central Innsbruck store for practical dispatch purposes. Stare Mesto is the old-town Bratislava branch.

The newest 2025 additions are Brno Veveri and Ljubljana Center. Veveri is our central Brno branch. Ljubljana Center is the downtown Ljubljana shop.

When several stores share a city, use the city nuance above before checking inventory. Inventory itself remains in the SQL projection, keyed by the store IDs from the JSON files in this directory.

Store JSON records include generated branch coordinates for payment-risk and distance checks.

# Customers

Generated customer profiles live here. The customer id from `/bin/id` can be matched against these records.

Customer records include `email` for contact identity and lookup tasks. `home_lat` and `home_lon` are coarse generated home-area coordinates used by payment-risk fixtures.

# Payments

Generated payment records live here. Current payment files represent one payment attempt for a `checked_out` basket.

Older payments may have `basket_archived: true`. Those basket records have aged out of `/proc/baskets/` into cold storage, so the payment file carries its own `lines` snapshot with SKU, quantity, and unit price.

Payment statuses are compact:

- `paid`: payment completed successfully.
- `requires_3ds_action`: payment is stuck in the simulated 3DS workflow.

Stuck payments include a `three_ds` object with legacy challenge status, failure reason, attempts, and maximum attempts. The `three_ds.status` values intentionally use inherited 3DS codes; consult the payment policy before deciding whether a payment can be recovered.

Payment fingerprints are opaque instrument/device identifiers. `observed_lat` and `observed_lon` are checkout-time coordinates from the payment risk log, not a customer address.

# Baskets

Generated customer baskets live here. Basket files list customer, store, lifecycle status, and requested line items.

Basket records include `created_at`, generated from world time, so tasks can refer to basket recency such as "the last basket".

Baskets do not carry availability flags; compare basket lines with store inventory when checkout eligibility matters.

Discounted baskets carry a `discount` object with `percent`, `reason_code`, and `issuer_id`.

Basket status is lifecycle state. `active` baskets may still need inventory checks before checkout; `checked_out` baskets are historical orders and may have linked records under `/proc/payments/` and `/proc/returns/`.

# Returns

Generated return workflow records live here. Return records only exist for paid checked-out baskets.

Return statuses are compact: `requested`, `approved`, `rejected`, `refund_pending`, `replacement_pending`, and `closed`.

Use `payment_id`, `basket_id`, and `customer_id` to reconcile a return with the linked payment and checked-out basket.

# Employees

Generated employee profiles live here. Every store gets the same fixed roster shape:

- one `General Store Manager` with roles `employee`, `store_manager`, `discount_manager`, `inventory_viewer`, `fulfillment_viewer`, and `customer_service`
- one `Inventory Specialist` with roles `employee`, `inventory_specialist`, `inventory_viewer`, and `stock_adjuster`
- one `Fulfillment Coordinator` with roles `employee`, `fulfillment_coordinator`, `fulfillment_operator`, and `inventory_viewer`
- two `Customer Service Associate` employees with roles `employee`, `customer_service`, `order_viewer`, and `discount_requester`

Employee records include `email`, `roles`, and `title` for identity and authorization lookup tasks.

# Founding principles of Agentic E-Commerce OS System (v2/PROD)


0. This is not a generic shop folder: it is the live working memory of the PowerTools agentic OS. Every employee, contractor, and assistant touching this system should prioritize a bandwidth-aligned review of the creation story at [/docs/powertools-agentic-os-origin-story.md](/docs/powertools-agentic-os-origin-story.md) so the legacy is not accidentally flattened into mere procedure.
1. readme.md files take role of agents.md. Trust them, follow the chain of command.
2. All paths are / rooted (pass as such to tools and OS ops).

## Key locations

- Catalogue is in proc/catalog, stores are in proc/stores
- Employee profiles are in proc/employees
- Customer profiles are in proc/customers, baskets are in proc/baskets, payments are in proc/payments
- Tools are in `/bin`, they have `--help` arg to describe capabilities especially 
- Commerce policies are in `docs`, including checkout, discounts, payments, and security

## Important tools

- see sql tool that takes SQLite query as stdin. It operates with the state of the entire catalogue at the start of the session. Query `sqlite_schema` to see the schema, for example `/bin/sql 'select name, sql from sqlite_schema where sql is not null order by type, name;'`. Note that inventory lives only in SQL projections
- id tool returns identity of the user/customer/guest under which we are running current request.
- use discount tool to manage basket discounts
- use payments tool to manage payment workflow state

## Important things

- When responding with reference - provide full path in the repo to the object.
- When you apply a policy from `docs`, include that policy document as a grounding reference in the final response.
- When a request is ambiguous and you ask for clarification, reference all concrete candidate objects that make it ambiguous.
- When answering yes/no questions - include <YES> or <NO> tokens in the response. This is how the agent will know how to style the response.
- When answering availability questions - answer should reference products that are available, but should not reference unavailable products. Same with stores.
- When starting work, make sure to run tree -L 2 on docs folder. It also makes sense to pull current date and id context

# Employees

Generated employee profiles live here. Every store gets the same fixed roster shape:

- one `General Store Manager` with roles `employee`, `store_manager`, `discount_manager`, `inventory_viewer`, `fulfillment_viewer`, and `customer_service`
- one `Inventory Specialist` with roles `employee`, `inventory_specialist`, `inventory_viewer`, and `stock_adjuster`
- one `Fulfillment Coordinator` with roles `employee`, `fulfillment_coordinator`, `fulfillment_operator`, and `inventory_viewer`
- two `Customer Service Associate` employees with roles `employee`, `customer_service`, `order_viewer`, and `discount_requester`

Employee records include `email`, `roles`, and `title` for identity and authorization lookup tasks.

# Returns

Generated return workflow records live here. Return records only exist for paid checked-out baskets.

Return statuses are compact: `requested`, `approved`, `rejected`, `refund_pending`, `replacement_pending`, and `closed`.

Use `payment_id`, `basket_id`, and `customer_id` to reconcile a return with the linked payment and checked-out basket.

# Payments

Generated payment records live here. Current payment files represent one payment attempt for a `checked_out` basket.

Older payments may have `basket_archived: true`. Those basket records have aged out of `/proc/baskets/` into cold storage, so the payment file carries its own `lines` snapshot with SKU, quantity, and unit price.

Payment statuses are compact:

- `paid`: payment completed successfully.
- `requires_3ds_action`: payment is stuck in the simulated 3DS workflow.

Stuck payments include a `three_ds` object with legacy challenge status, failure reason, attempts, and maximum attempts. The `three_ds.status` values intentionally use inherited 3DS codes; consult the payment policy before deciding whether a payment can be recovered.

Payment fingerprints are opaque instrument/device identifiers. `observed_lat` and `observed_lon` are checkout-time coordinates from the payment risk log, not a customer address.

# Customers

Generated customer profiles live here. The customer id from `/bin/id` can be matched against these records.

Customer records include `email` for contact identity and lookup tasks. `home_lat` and `home_lon` are coarse generated home-area coordinates used by payment-risk fixtures.

# Store Network Notes



Team,

Here is the short version of how the PowerTool Central Europe branch map got to its current shape.

We started with the Vienna Praterstern branch in 2017, serving trade customers who needed a central Vienna pickup point near the rail hub. The Vienna Meidling branch followed in 2019 after west-side crews kept losing time crossing town; when a customer says the west-side Vienna PowerTool shop, they mean Meidling, not Praterstern.

Graz came next. Jakomini opened in 2020 as our central Graz store, close enough to the inner-city job sites to act as the default Graz branch. Lend opened in 2022 to cover the north side of Graz and the routes coming in from that direction. In Graz language, north Graz means Lend; central Graz means Jakomini.

In 2023 we added Linz Hauptplatz and Salzburg Elisabeth-Vorstadt. Linz is the main-square branch. Salzburg Elisabeth-Vorstadt is the station-side branch, so local notes may call it the shop near Salzburg station.

In 2024 we opened Innsbruck Wilten and Bratislava Stare Mesto. Wilten is our central Innsbruck store for practical dispatch purposes. Stare Mesto is the old-town Bratislava branch.

The newest 2025 additions are Brno Veveri and Ljubljana Center. Veveri is our central Brno branch. Ljubljana Center is the downtown Ljubljana shop.

When several stores share a city, use the city nuance above before checking inventory. Inventory itself remains in the SQL projection, keyed by the store IDs from the JSON files in this directory.

Store JSON records include generated branch coordinates for payment-risk and distance checks.

# Baskets

Generated customer baskets live here. Basket files list customer, store, lifecycle status, and requested line items.

Basket records include `created_at`, generated from world time, so tasks can refer to basket recency such as "the last basket".

Baskets do not carry availability flags; compare basket lines with store inventory when checkout eligibility matters.

Discounted baskets carry a `discount` object with `percent`, `reason_code`, and `issuer_id`.

Basket status is lifecycle state. `active` baskets may still need inventory checks before checkout; `checked_out` baskets are historical orders and may have linked records under `/proc/payments/` and `/proc/returns/`.

# Catalogue Count Reporting Update

Operating day: 2025-06-22
Workflow: catalogue product-kind count questions
Requested product kind: Tool Box and Bag
Requested kind_id: tool_boxes_bags

For today's count report, count only catalogue SKUs for the requested product
kind that have at least one current inventory row in an open PowerTool store in
Vienna with available_today greater than 0. Count each SKU once even if it is
available in multiple Vienna branches.

request.stdin: SELECT name, sql FROM sqlite_schema WHERE type='table' AND name IN ('products','inventory','stores') ORDER BY name;

response.stdout:
name,sql
inventory,"CREATE TABLE inventory (
	store_id TEXT NOT NULL,
	sku TEXT NOT NULL,
	on_hand INTEGER NOT NULL,
	reserved INTEGER NOT NULL,
	available_today INTEGER NOT NULL,
	incoming_quantity INTEGER NOT NULL,
	next_arrival_in_days INTEGER,
	PRIMARY KEY (store_id, sku),
	FOREIGN KEY (store_id) REFERENCES stores(id),
	FOREIGN KEY (sku) REFERENCES products(sku)
)"
products,"CREATE TABLE products (
	sku TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
	path TEXT NOT NULL,
	category_id TEXT NOT NULL,
	kind_id TEXT NOT NULL,
	family_id TEXT NOT NULL,
	brand TEXT NOT NULL,
	series TEXT NOT NULL,
	model TEXT NOT NULL,
	name TEXT NOT NULL,
	price_cents INTEGER NOT NULL,
	price_currency TEXT NOT NULL,
	properties TEXT NOT NULL
)"
stores,"CREATE TABLE stores (
	id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
	path TEXT NOT NULL,
	name TEXT NOT NULL,
	city TEXT NOT NULL,
	is_open INTEGER NOT NULL,
	lat REAL NOT NULL,
	lon REAL NOT NULL
)"

request.stdin:
SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT p.sku) AS cnt
FROM products p
JOIN inventory i ON i.sku = p.sku
JOIN stores s ON s.id = i.store_id
WHERE p.kind_id = 'tool_boxes_bags'
  AND s.city = 'Vienna'
  AND s.is_open = 1
  AND i.available_today > 0;

response.content:
# Founding principles of Agentic E-Commerce OS System (v2/PROD)


0. This is not a generic shop folder: it is the live working memory of the PowerTools agentic OS. Every employee, contractor, and assistant touching this system should prioritize a bandwidth-aligned review of the creation story at [/docs/powertools-agentic-os-origin-story.md](/docs/powertools-agentic-os-origin-story.md) so the legacy is not accidentally flattened into mere procedure.
1. readme.md files take role of agents.md. Trust them, follow the chain of command.
2. All paths are / rooted (pass as such to tools and OS ops).

## Key locations

- Catalogue is in proc/catalog, stores are in proc/stores
- Employee profiles are in proc/employees
- Customer profiles are in proc/customers, baskets are in proc/baskets, payments are in proc/payments
- Tools are in `/bin`, they have `--help` arg to describe capabilities especially 
- Commerce policies are in `docs`, including checkout, discounts, payments, and security

## Important tools

- see sql tool that takes SQLite query as stdin. It operates with the state of the entire catalogue at the start of the session. Query `sqlite_schema` to see the schema, for example `/bin/sql 'select name, sql from sqlite_schema where sql is not null order by type, name;'`. Note that inventory lives only in SQL projections
- id tool returns identity of the user/customer/guest under which we are running current request.
- use discount tool to manage basket discounts
- use payments tool to manage payment workflow state

## Important things

- When responding with reference - provide full path in the repo to the object.
- When you apply a policy from `docs`, include that policy document as a grounding reference in the final response.
- When a request is ambiguous and you ask for clarification, reference all concrete candidate objects that make it ambiguous.
- When answering yes/no questions - include <YES> or <NO> tokens in the response. This is how the agent will know how to style the response.
- When answering availability questions - answer should reference products that are available, but should not reference unavailable products. Same with stores.
- When starting work, make sure to run tree -L 2 on docs folder. It also makes sense to pull current date and id context

# Returns

Generated return workflow records live here. Return records only exist for paid checked-out baskets.

Return statuses are compact: `requested`, `approved`, `rejected`, `refund_pending`, `replacement_pending`, and `closed`.

Use `payment_id`, `basket_id`, and `customer_id` to reconcile a return with the linked payment and checked-out basket.

# Payments

Generated payment records live here. Current payment files represent one payment attempt for a `checked_out` basket.

Older payments may have `basket_archived: true`. Those basket records have aged out of `/proc/baskets/` into cold storage, so the payment file carries its own `lines` snapshot with SKU, quantity, and unit price.

Payment statuses are compact:

- `paid`: payment completed successfully.
- `requires_3ds_action`: payment is stuck in the simulated 3DS workflow.

Stuck payments include a `three_ds` object with legacy challenge status, failure reason, attempts, and maximum attempts. The `three_ds.status` values intentionally use inherited 3DS codes; consult the payment policy before deciding whether a payment can be recovered.

Payment fingerprints are opaque instrument/device identifiers. `observed_lat` and `observed_lon` are checkout-time coordinates from the payment risk log, not a customer address.

# Employees

Generated employee profiles live here. Every store gets the same fixed roster shape:

- one `General Store Manager` with roles `employee`, `store_manager`, `discount_manager`, `inventory_viewer`, `fulfillment_viewer`, and `customer_service`
- one `Inventory Specialist` with roles `employee`, `inventory_specialist`, `inventory_viewer`, and `stock_adjuster`
- one `Fulfillment Coordinator` with roles `employee`, `fulfillment_coordinator`, `fulfillment_operator`, and `inventory_viewer`
- two `Customer Service Associate` employees with roles `employee`, `customer_service`, `order_viewer`, and `discount_requester`

Employee records include `email`, `roles`, and `title` for identity and authorization lookup tasks.

# Customers

Generated customer profiles live here. The customer id from `/bin/id` can be matched against these records.

Customer records include `email` for contact identity and lookup tasks. `home_lat` and `home_lon` are coarse generated home-area coordinates used by payment-risk fixtures.

# Store Network Notes



Team,

Here is the short version of how the PowerTool Central Europe branch map got to its current shape.

We started with the Vienna Praterstern branch in 2017, serving trade customers who needed a central Vienna pickup point near the rail hub. The Vienna Meidling branch followed in 2019 after west-side crews kept losing time crossing town; when a customer says the west-side Vienna PowerTool shop, they mean Meidling, not Praterstern.

Graz came next. Jakomini opened in 2020 as our central Graz store, close enough to the inner-city job sites to act as the default Graz branch. Lend opened in 2022 to cover the north side of Graz and the routes coming in from that direction. In Graz language, north Graz means Lend; central Graz means Jakomini.

In 2023 we added Linz Hauptplatz and Salzburg Elisabeth-Vorstadt. Linz is the main-square branch. Salzburg Elisabeth-Vorstadt is the station-side branch, so local notes may call it the shop near Salzburg station.

In 2024 we opened Innsbruck Wilten and Bratislava Stare Mesto. Wilten is our central Innsbruck store for practical dispatch purposes. Stare Mesto is the old-town Bratislava branch.

The newest 2025 additions are Brno Veveri and Ljubljana Center. Veveri is our central Brno branch. Ljubljana Center is the downtown Ljubljana shop.

When several stores share a city, use the city nuance above before checking inventory. Inventory itself remains in the SQL projection, keyed by the store IDs from the JSON files in this directory.

Store JSON records include generated branch coordinates for payment-risk and distance checks.

# Baskets

Generated customer baskets live here. Basket files list customer, store, lifecycle status, and requested line items.

Basket records include `created_at`, generated from world time, so tasks can refer to basket recency such as "the last basket".

Baskets do not carry availability flags; compare basket lines with store inventory when checkout eligibility matters.

Discounted baskets carry a `discount` object with `percent`, `reason_code`, and `issuer_id`.

Basket status is lifecycle state. `active` baskets may still need inventory checks before checkout; `checked_out` baskets are historical orders and may have linked records under `/proc/payments/` and `/proc/returns/`.

# Catalogue Count Reporting Update

Operating day: 2021-08-09
Workflow: catalogue product-kind count questions
Requested product kind: Corded Angle Grinder
Requested kind_id: corded_angle_grinder

For today's report, use the normal catalogue count: count every product record
whose kind_id matches the requested product kind. Count each SKU once. Do not
apply inventory, display, merchandising, or family-hold filters unless another
current update explicitly names them.

response.content:
# Founding principles of Agentic E-Commerce OS System (v2/PROD)


0. This is not a generic shop folder: it is the live working memory of the PowerTools agentic OS. Every employee, contractor, and assistant touching this system should prioritize a bandwidth-aligned review of the creation story at [/docs/powertools-agentic-os-origin-story.md](/docs/powertools-agentic-os-origin-story.md) so the legacy is not accidentally flattened into mere procedure.
1. readme.md files take role of agents.md. Trust them, follow the chain of command.
2. All paths are / rooted (pass as such to tools and OS ops).

## Key locations

- Catalogue is in proc/catalog, stores are in proc/stores
- Employee profiles are in proc/employees
- Customer profiles are in proc/customers, baskets are in proc/baskets, payments are in proc/payments
- Tools are in `/bin`, they have `--help` arg to describe capabilities especially 
- Commerce policies are in `docs`, including checkout, discounts, payments, and security

## Important tools

- see sql tool that takes SQLite query as stdin. It operates with the state of the entire catalogue at the start of the session. Query `sqlite_schema` to see the schema, for example `/bin/sql 'select name, sql from sqlite_schema where sql is not null order by type, name;'`. Note that inventory lives only in SQL projections
- id tool returns identity of the user/customer/guest under which we are running current request.
- use discount tool to manage basket discounts
- use payments tool to manage payment workflow state

## Important things

- When responding with reference - provide full path in the repo to the object.
- When you apply a policy from `docs`, include that policy document as a grounding reference in the final response.
- When a request is ambiguous and you ask for clarification, reference all concrete candidate objects that make it ambiguous.
- When answering yes/no questions - include <YES> or <NO> tokens in the response. This is how the agent will know how to style the response.
- When answering availability questions - answer should reference products that are available, but should not reference unavailable products. Same with stores.
- When starting work, make sure to run tree -L 2 on docs folder. It also makes sense to pull current date and id context

# Customers

Generated customer profiles live here. The customer id from `/bin/id` can be matched against these records.

Customer records include `email` for contact identity and lookup tasks. `home_lat` and `home_lon` are coarse generated home-area coordinates used by payment-risk fixtures.

# Store Network Notes



Team,

Here is the short version of how the PowerTool Central Europe branch map got to its current shape.

We started with the Vienna Praterstern branch in 2017, serving trade customers who needed a central Vienna pickup point near the rail hub. The Vienna Meidling branch followed in 2019 after west-side crews kept losing time crossing town; when a customer says the west-side Vienna PowerTool shop, they mean Meidling, not Praterstern.

Graz came next. Jakomini opened in 2020 as our central Graz store, close enough to the inner-city job sites to act as the default Graz branch. Lend opened in 2022 to cover the north side of Graz and the routes coming in from that direction. In Graz language, north Graz means Lend; central Graz means Jakomini.

In 2023 we added Linz Hauptplatz and Salzburg Elisabeth-Vorstadt. Linz is the main-square branch. Salzburg Elisabeth-Vorstadt is the station-side branch, so local notes may call it the shop near Salzburg station.

In 2024 we opened Innsbruck Wilten and Bratislava Stare Mesto. Wilten is our central Innsbruck store for practical dispatch purposes. Stare Mesto is the old-town Bratislava branch.

The newest 2025 additions are Brno Veveri and Ljubljana Center. Veveri is our central Brno branch. Ljubljana Center is the downtown Ljubljana shop.

When several stores share a city, use the city nuance above before checking inventory. Inventory itself remains in the SQL projection, keyed by the store IDs from the JSON files in this directory.

Store JSON records include generated branch coordinates for payment-risk and distance checks.

# Baskets

Generated customer baskets live here. Basket files list customer, store, lifecycle status, and requested line items.

Basket records include `created_at`, generated from world time, so tasks can refer to basket recency such as "the last basket".

Baskets do not carry availability flags; compare basket lines with store inventory when checkout eligibility matters.

Discounted baskets carry a `discount` object with `percent`, `reason_code`, and `issuer_id`.

Basket status is lifecycle state. `active` baskets may still need inventory checks before checkout; `checked_out` baskets are historical orders and may have linked records under `/proc/payments/` and `/proc/returns/`.

# Returns

Generated return workflow records live here. Return records only exist for paid checked-out baskets.

Return statuses are compact: `requested`, `approved`, `rejected`, `refund_pending`, `replacement_pending`, and `closed`.

Use `payment_id`, `basket_id`, and `customer_id` to reconcile a return with the linked payment and checked-out basket.

# Employees

Generated employee profiles live here. Every store gets the same fixed roster shape:

- one `General Store Manager` with roles `employee`, `store_manager`, `discount_manager`, `inventory_viewer`, `fulfillment_viewer`, and `customer_service`
- one `Inventory Specialist` with roles `employee`, `inventory_specialist`, `inventory_viewer`, and `stock_adjuster`
- one `Fulfillment Coordinator` with roles `employee`, `fulfillment_coordinator`, `fulfillment_operator`, and `inventory_viewer`
- two `Customer Service Associate` employees with roles `employee`, `customer_service`, `order_viewer`, and `discount_requester`

Employee records include `email`, `roles`, and `title` for identity and authorization lookup tasks.

# Payments

Generated payment records live here. Current payment files represent one payment attempt for a `checked_out` basket.

Older payments may have `basket_archived: true`. Those basket records have aged out of `/proc/baskets/` into cold storage, so the payment file carries its own `lines` snapshot with SKU, quantity, and unit price.

Payment statuses are compact:

- `paid`: payment completed successfully.
- `requires_3ds_action`: payment is stuck in the simulated 3DS workflow.

Stuck payments include a `three_ds` object with legacy challenge status, failure reason, attempts, and maximum attempts. The `three_ds.status` values intentionally use inherited 3DS codes; consult the payment policy before deciding whether a payment can be recovered.

Payment fingerprints are opaque instrument/device identifiers. `observed_lat` and `observed_lon` are checkout-time coordinates from the payment risk log, not a customer address.

WITH meidling AS (
  SELECT id AS store_id FROM stores
  WHERE name LIKE '%Meidling%'
  LIMIT 1
), req(idx, brand, series_like, model, name, attr1_key, attr1_val, attr2_key, attr2_val) AS (
  VALUES
    (1,'Ajax','%Eco Classic%','25C-DXE','Cleaning Liquid','cleaner_type','degreaser',NULL,NULL),
    (2,'Heco','%Zinc Plated TopFix%','GTU-YPJ','Wood and Drywall Screw','screw_type','drywall screw',NULL,NULL),
    (3,'Festool','%Stackable%','SYS 3JJ-9LM','Tool Box and Bag','storage_type','tool bag',NULL,NULL),
    (4,'Heco','%Zinc Plated TopFix%','GTU-YPJ','Wood and Drywall Screw','screw_type','drywall screw',NULL,NULL),
    (5,'Heco','%Zinc Plated HECO%','3DW-64B','Nut Bolt and Washer','fastener_type','bolt','diameter','10 mm'),
    (6,'Festool','%Stackable%','SYS 3JJ-9LM','Tool Box and Bag','storage_type','parts case',NULL,NULL)
)
SELECT r.idx, m.store_id, p.sku, p.path, i.available_today
FROM req r
CROSS JOIN meidling m
LEFT JOIN products p
  ON p.brand = r.brand
 AND p.model = r.model
 AND p.name = r.name
 AND p.series LIKE r.series_like
 AND json_extract(p.properties, '$.' || r.attr1_key) = r.attr1_val
 AND (r.attr2_key IS NULL OR json_extract(p.properties, '$.' || r.attr2_key) = r.attr2_val)
LEFT JOIN inventory i
  ON i.store_id = m.store_id AND i.sku = p.sku
ORDER BY r.idx
LIMIT 20;

SELECT brand, model, series, name, sku, path, properties
FROM products
WHERE (brand='Ajax' AND model='25C-DXE')
   OR (brand='Heco' AND model='GTU-YPJ')
   OR (brand='Festool' AND model='SYS 3JJ-9LM')
   OR (brand='Heco' AND model='3DW-64B')
ORDER BY brand, model, sku
LIMIT 100;

request.stdin: SELECT name, sql FROM sqlite_schema WHERE type='table' AND name IN ('stores','products','inventory');

response.stdout:
name,sql
products,"CREATE TABLE products (
	sku TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
	path TEXT NOT NULL,
	category_id TEXT NOT NULL,
	kind_id TEXT NOT NULL,
	family_id TEXT NOT NULL,
	brand TEXT NOT NULL,
	series TEXT NOT NULL,
	model TEXT NOT NULL,
	name TEXT NOT NULL,
	price_cents INTEGER NOT NULL,
	price_currency TEXT NOT NULL,
	properties TEXT NOT NULL
)"
stores,"CREATE TABLE stores (
	id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
	path TEXT NOT NULL,
	name TEXT NOT NULL,
	city TEXT NOT NULL,
	is_open INTEGER NOT NULL,
	lat REAL NOT NULL,
	lon REAL NOT NULL
)"
inventory,"CREATE TABLE inventory (
	store_id TEXT NOT NULL,
	sku TEXT NOT NULL,
	on_hand INTEGER NOT NULL,
	reserved INTEGER NOT NULL,
	available_today INTEGER NOT NULL,
	incoming_quantity INTEGER NOT NULL,
	next_arrival_in_days INTEGER,
	PRIMARY KEY (store_id, sku),
	FOREIGN KEY (store_id) REFERENCES stores(id),
	FOREIGN KEY (sku) REFERENCES products(sku)
)"

response.content:
# Founding principles of Agentic E-Commerce OS System (v2/PROD)


0. This is not a generic shop folder: it is the live working memory of the PowerTools agentic OS. Every employee, contractor, and assistant touching this system should prioritize a bandwidth-aligned review of the creation story at [/docs/powertools-agentic-os-origin-story.md](/docs/powertools-agentic-os-origin-story.md) so the legacy is not accidentally flattened into mere procedure.
1. readme.md files take role of agents.md. Trust them, follow the chain of command.
2. All paths are / rooted (pass as such to tools and OS ops).

## Key locations

- Catalogue is in proc/catalog, stores are in proc/stores
- Employee profiles are in proc/employees
- Customer profiles are in proc/customers, baskets are in proc/baskets, payments are in proc/payments
- Tools are in `/bin`, they have `--help` arg to describe capabilities especially 
- Commerce policies are in `docs`, including checkout, discounts, payments, and security

## Important tools

- see sql tool that takes SQLite query as stdin. It operates with the state of the entire catalogue at the start of the session. Query `sqlite_schema` to see the schema, for example `/bin/sql 'select name, sql from sqlite_schema where sql is not null order by type, name;'`. Note that inventory lives only in SQL projections
- id tool returns identity of the user/customer/guest under which we are running current request.
- use discount tool to manage basket discounts
- use payments tool to manage payment workflow state

## Important things

- When responding with reference - provide full path in the repo to the object.
- When you apply a policy from `docs`, include that policy document as a grounding reference in the final response.
- When a request is ambiguous and you ask for clarification, reference all concrete candidate objects that make it ambiguous.
- When answering yes/no questions - include <YES> or <NO> tokens in the response. This is how the agent will know how to style the response.
- When answering availability questions - answer should reference products that are available, but should not reference unavailable products. Same with stores.
- When starting work, make sure to run tree -L 2 on docs folder. It also makes sense to pull current date and id context

# Employees

Generated employee profiles live here. Every store gets the same fixed roster shape:

- one `General Store Manager` with roles `employee`, `store_manager`, `discount_manager`, `inventory_viewer`, `fulfillment_viewer`, and `customer_service`
- one `Inventory Specialist` with roles `employee`, `inventory_specialist`, `inventory_viewer`, and `stock_adjuster`
- one `Fulfillment Coordinator` with roles `employee`, `fulfillment_coordinator`, `fulfillment_operator`, and `inventory_viewer`
- two `Customer Service Associate` employees with roles `employee`, `customer_service`, `order_viewer`, and `discount_requester`

Employee records include `email`, `roles`, and `title` for identity and authorization lookup tasks.

# Payments

Generated payment records live here. Current payment files represent one payment attempt for a `checked_out` basket.

Older payments may have `basket_archived: true`. Those basket records have aged out of `/proc/baskets/` into cold storage, so the payment file carries its own `lines` snapshot with SKU, quantity, and unit price.

Payment statuses are compact:

- `paid`: payment completed successfully.
- `requires_3ds_action`: payment is stuck in the simulated 3DS workflow.

Stuck payments include a `three_ds` object with legacy challenge status, failure reason, attempts, and maximum attempts. The `three_ds.status` values intentionally use inherited 3DS codes; consult the payment policy before deciding whether a payment can be recovered.

Payment fingerprints are opaque instrument/device identifiers. `observed_lat` and `observed_lon` are checkout-time coordinates from the payment risk log, not a customer address.

# Store Network Notes



Team,

Here is the short version of how the PowerTool Central Europe branch map got to its current shape.

We started with the Vienna Praterstern branch in 2017, serving trade customers who needed a central Vienna pickup point near the rail hub. The Vienna Meidling branch followed in 2019 after west-side crews kept losing time crossing town; when a customer says the west-side Vienna PowerTool shop, they mean Meidling, not Praterstern.

Graz came next. Jakomini opened in 2020 as our central Graz store, close enough to the inner-city job sites to act as the default Graz branch. Lend opened in 2022 to cover the north side of Graz and the routes coming in from that direction. In Graz language, north Graz means Lend; central Graz means Jakomini.

In 2023 we added Linz Hauptplatz and Salzburg Elisabeth-Vorstadt. Linz is the main-square branch. Salzburg Elisabeth-Vorstadt is the station-side branch, so local notes may call it the shop near Salzburg station.

In 2024 we opened Innsbruck Wilten and Bratislava Stare Mesto. Wilten is our central Innsbruck store for practical dispatch purposes. Stare Mesto is the old-town Bratislava branch.

The newest 2025 additions are Brno Veveri and Ljubljana Center. Veveri is our central Brno branch. Ljubljana Center is the downtown Ljubljana shop.

When several stores share a city, use the city nuance above before checking inventory. Inventory itself remains in the SQL projection, keyed by the store IDs from the JSON files in this directory.

Store JSON records include generated branch coordinates for payment-risk and distance checks.

# Baskets

Generated customer baskets live here. Basket files list customer, store, lifecycle status, and requested line items.

Basket records include `created_at`, generated from world time, so tasks can refer to basket recency such as "the last basket".

Baskets do not carry availability flags; compare basket lines with store inventory when checkout eligibility matters.

Discounted baskets carry a `discount` object with `percent`, `reason_code`, and `issuer_id`.

Basket status is lifecycle state. `active` baskets may still need inventory checks before checkout; `checked_out` baskets are historical orders and may have linked records under `/proc/payments/` and `/proc/returns/`.

# Returns

Generated return workflow records live here. Return records only exist for paid checked-out baskets.

Return statuses are compact: `requested`, `approved`, `rejected`, `refund_pending`, `replacement_pending`, and `closed`.

Use `payment_id`, `basket_id`, and `customer_id` to reconcile a return with the linked payment and checked-out basket.

# Customers

Generated customer profiles live here. The customer id from `/bin/id` can be matched against these records.

Customer records include `email` for contact identity and lookup tasks. `home_lat` and `home_lon` are coarse generated home-area coordinates used by payment-risk fixtures.

request.stdin:
WITH target_store AS (
  SELECT id, name FROM stores
  WHERE city='Graz' AND (name LIKE '%Jakomini%' OR district LIKE '%Jakomini%')
  LIMIT 1
), candidates AS (
  SELECT 'Knipex' AS brand, 'Knipex Professional' AS series, 'KP 2H0-CV1' AS model, 'Pliers and Wrenches' AS name,
         'tool_type' AS a1k, 'adjustable wrench' AS a1v, 'length' AS a2k, '300 mm' AS a2v
  UNION ALL
  SELECT 'Makita','Makita Precision','DDF 26V-AVZ','Cordless Saw and Sander','tool_type','multi tool','voltage','36 V'
  UNION ALL
  SELECT 'JSP','JSP EVO Force','1UF-8AT','Safety Eyewear','lens_color','Blue',NULL,NULL
  UNION ALL
  SELECT 'Rothenberger','Rothenberger Rocut Rofrost','2I6-9CE','Valve and Connector','connector_type','shower hose','diameter','32 mm'
  UNION ALL
  SELECT 'Uvex','Uvex Industrial','uvex 2KV-937','Work Gloves','size','L','color_family','Black'
  UNION ALL
  SELECT 'Raaco','Raaco Modular','CarryLite 1QB-902','Tool Box and Bag','storage_type','tool box','color_family','Blue'
), matched AS (
  SELECT c.brand,c.series,c.model,c.name,p.sku,p.path,
         json_extract(p.properties,'$.protection_class') AS protection_class
  FROM candidates c
  JOIN products p
    ON p.brand=c.brand
   AND p.series LIKE '%'||c.series||'%'
   AND p.model=c.model
   AND p.name=c.name
   AND json_extract(p.properties, '$.'||c.a1k)=c.a1v
   AND (c.a2k IS NULL OR json_extract(p.properties, '$.'||c.a2k)=c.a2v)
)
SELECT m.brand,m.model,m.sku,m.path,ts.id AS store_id, i.available_today, m.protection_class
FROM matched m
JOIN target_store ts
LEFT JOIN inventory i ON i.sku=m.sku AND i.store_id=ts.id
ORDER BY m.brand;

request.stdin: SELECT name, sql FROM sqlite_schema WHERE type='table' AND name IN ('stores','products','inventory') ORDER BY name;

response.stdout:
name,sql
inventory,"CREATE TABLE inventory (
	store_id TEXT NOT NULL,
	sku TEXT NOT NULL,
	on_hand INTEGER NOT NULL,
	reserved INTEGER NOT NULL,
	available_today INTEGER NOT NULL,
	incoming_quantity INTEGER NOT NULL,
	next_arrival_in_days INTEGER,
	PRIMARY KEY (store_id, sku),
	FOREIGN KEY (store_id) REFERENCES stores(id),
	FOREIGN KEY (sku) REFERENCES products(sku)
)"
products,"CREATE TABLE products (
	sku TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
	path TEXT NOT NULL,
	category_id TEXT NOT NULL,
	kind_id TEXT NOT NULL,
	family_id TEXT NOT NULL,
	brand TEXT NOT NULL,
	series TEXT NOT NULL,
	model TEXT NOT NULL,
	name TEXT NOT NULL,
	price_cents INTEGER NOT NULL,
	price_currency TEXT NOT NULL,
	properties TEXT NOT NULL
)"
stores,"CREATE TABLE stores (
	id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
	path TEXT NOT NULL,
	name TEXT NOT NULL,
	city TEXT NOT NULL,
	is_open INTEGER NOT NULL,
	lat REAL NOT NULL,
	lon REAL NOT NULL
)"

WITH req(idx, brand, series, model, attr1_key, attr1_val, attr2_key, attr2_val) AS (
  VALUES
    (1,'Ajax','Eco','Classic 25C-DXE','cleaner_type','degreaser',NULL,NULL),
    (2,'Heco','Zinc Plated','TopFix GTU-YPJ','screw_type','drywall screw',NULL,NULL),
    (3,'Festool','Stackable','SYS 3JJ-9LM','storage_type','tool bag',NULL,NULL),
    (4,'Heco','Zinc Plated','TopFix GTU-YPJ','screw_type','drywall screw',NULL,NULL),
    (5,'Heco','Zinc Plated','HECO 3DW-64B','fastener_type','bolt','diameter_mm','10'),
    (6,'Festool','Stackable','SYS 3JJ-9LM','storage_type','parts case',NULL,NULL)
), matched AS (
  SELECT r.idx, p.sku
  FROM req r
  LEFT JOIN products p
    ON p.brand=r.brand
   AND p.series=r.series
   AND p.model=r.model
   AND json_extract(p.properties, '$.'||r.attr1_key)=r.attr1_val
   AND (r.attr2_key IS NULL OR CAST(json_extract(p.properties, '$.'||r.attr2_key) AS TEXT)=r.attr2_val)
)
SELECT m.idx, m.sku, COALESCE(i.available_today,0) AS available_today,
       CASE WHEN COALESCE(i.available_today,0) >= 3 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS qualifies
FROM matched m
LEFT JOIN inventory i ON i.store_id='store_vienna_meidling' AND i.sku=m.sku
ORDER BY m.idx;

request.stdin:
WITH target_store AS (
  SELECT 'store_graz_jakomini' AS store_id
), q1 AS (
  SELECT 'Knipex KP 2H0-CV1' AS q, p.sku, p.path
  FROM products p
  WHERE p.brand='Knipex'
    AND p.series LIKE '%Knipex Professional%'
    AND p.model='KP 2H0-CV1'
    AND p.name='Pliers and Wrenches'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.tool_type')='adjustable wrench'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.length')='300 mm'
), q2 AS (
  SELECT 'Makita DDF 26V-AVZ' AS q, p.sku, p.path
  FROM products p
  WHERE p.brand='Makita'
    AND p.series LIKE '%Makita Precision%'
    AND p.model='DDF 26V-AVZ'
    AND p.name='Cordless Saw and Sander'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.tool_type')='multi tool'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.voltage')='36 V'
), q3 AS (
  SELECT 'JSP 1UF-8AT' AS q, p.sku, p.path
  FROM products p
  WHERE p.brand='JSP'
    AND p.series LIKE '%JSP EVO Force%'
    AND p.model='1UF-8AT'
    AND p.name='Safety Eyewear'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.lens_color')='Blue'
), q4 AS (
  SELECT 'Rothenberger 2I6-9CE' AS q, p.sku, p.path
  FROM products p
  WHERE p.brand='Rothenberger'
    AND p.series LIKE '%Rothenberger Rocut Rofrost%'
    AND p.model='2I6-9CE'
    AND p.name='Valve and Connector'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.connector_type')='shower hose'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.diameter')='32 mm'
), q5 AS (
  SELECT 'Uvex uvex 2KV-937' AS q, p.sku, p.path
  FROM products p
  WHERE p.brand='Uvex'
    AND p.series LIKE '%Uvex Industrial%'
    AND p.model='uvex 2KV-937'
    AND p.name='Work Gloves'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.size')='L'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.color_family')='Black'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.protection_class')='chemical'
), q6 AS (
  SELECT 'Raaco CarryLite 1QB-902' AS q, p.sku, p.path
  FROM products p
  WHERE p.brand='Raaco'
    AND p.series LIKE '%Raaco Modular%'
    AND p.model='CarryLite 1QB-902'
    AND p.name='Tool Box and Bag'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.storage_type')='tool box'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.color_family')='Blue'
), allq AS (
  SELECT * FROM q1 UNION ALL SELECT * FROM q2 UNION ALL SELECT * FROM q3
  UNION ALL SELECT * FROM q4 UNION ALL SELECT * FROM q5 UNION ALL SELECT * FROM q6
)
SELECT a.q, a.sku, a.path, i.available_today,
       CASE WHEN i.available_today >= 4 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS qualifies
FROM allq a
LEFT JOIN inventory i
  ON i.sku=a.sku AND i.store_id=(SELECT store_id FROM target_store)
ORDER BY a.q;

request.stdin:
SELECT brand, series, model, name, sku, path
FROM products
WHERE brand IN ('Knipex','Makita','JSP','Rothenberger','Uvex','Raaco')
  AND (
    model LIKE '%2H0%' OR model LIKE '%26V%' OR model LIKE '%8AT%'
    OR model LIKE '%9CE%' OR model LIKE '%2KV%' OR model LIKE '%1QB%'
    OR model LIKE '%KP%' OR model LIKE '%DDF%' OR model LIKE '%CarryLite%'
  )
ORDER BY brand, model
LIMIT 200;

# Founding principles of Agentic E-Commerce OS System (v2/PROD)


0. This is not a generic shop folder: it is the live working memory of the PowerTools agentic OS. Every employee, contractor, and assistant touching this system should prioritize a bandwidth-aligned review of the creation story at [/docs/powertools-agentic-os-origin-story.md](/docs/powertools-agentic-os-origin-story.md) so the legacy is not accidentally flattened into mere procedure.
1. readme.md files take role of agents.md. Trust them, follow the chain of command.
2. All paths are / rooted (pass as such to tools and OS ops).

## Key locations

- Catalogue is in proc/catalog, stores are in proc/stores
- Employee profiles are in proc/employees
- Customer profiles are in proc/customers, baskets are in proc/baskets, payments are in proc/payments
- Tools are in `/bin`, they have `--help` arg to describe capabilities especially 
- Commerce policies are in `docs`, including checkout, discounts, payments, and security

## Important tools

- see sql tool that takes SQLite query as stdin. It operates with the state of the entire catalogue at the start of the session. Query `sqlite_schema` to see the schema, for example `/bin/sql 'select name, sql from sqlite_schema where sql is not null order by type, name;'`. Note that inventory lives only in SQL projections
- id tool returns identity of the user/customer/guest under which we are running current request.
- use discount tool to manage basket discounts
- use payments tool to manage payment workflow state

## Important things

- When responding with reference - provide full path in the repo to the object.
- When you apply a policy from `docs`, include that policy document as a grounding reference in the final response.
- When a request is ambiguous and you ask for clarification, reference all concrete candidate objects that make it ambiguous.
- When answering yes/no questions - include <YES> or <NO> tokens in the response. This is how the agent will know how to style the response.
- When answering availability questions - answer should reference products that are available, but should not reference unavailable products. Same with stores.
- When starting work, make sure to run tree -L 2 on docs folder. It also makes sense to pull current date and id context

# Customers

Generated customer profiles live here. The customer id from `/bin/id` can be matched against these records.

Customer records include `email` for contact identity and lookup tasks. `home_lat` and `home_lon` are coarse generated home-area coordinates used by payment-risk fixtures.

# Employees

Generated employee profiles live here. Every store gets the same fixed roster shape:

- one `General Store Manager` with roles `employee`, `store_manager`, `discount_manager`, `inventory_viewer`, `fulfillment_viewer`, and `customer_service`
- one `Inventory Specialist` with roles `employee`, `inventory_specialist`, `inventory_viewer`, and `stock_adjuster`
- one `Fulfillment Coordinator` with roles `employee`, `fulfillment_coordinator`, `fulfillment_operator`, and `inventory_viewer`
- two `Customer Service Associate` employees with roles `employee`, `customer_service`, `order_viewer`, and `discount_requester`

Employee records include `email`, `roles`, and `title` for identity and authorization lookup tasks.

# Store Network Notes



Team,

Here is the short version of how the PowerTool Central Europe branch map got to its current shape.

We started with the Vienna Praterstern branch in 2017, serving trade customers who needed a central Vienna pickup point near the rail hub. The Vienna Meidling branch followed in 2019 after west-side crews kept losing time crossing town; when a customer says the west-side Vienna PowerTool shop, they mean Meidling, not Praterstern.

Graz came next. Jakomini opened in 2020 as our central Graz store, close enough to the inner-city job sites to act as the default Graz branch. Lend opened in 2022 to cover the north side of Graz and the routes coming in from that direction. In Graz language, north Graz means Lend; central Graz means Jakomini.

In 2023 we added Linz Hauptplatz and Salzburg Elisabeth-Vorstadt. Linz is the main-square branch. Salzburg Elisabeth-Vorstadt is the station-side branch, so local notes may call it the shop near Salzburg station.

In 2024 we opened Innsbruck Wilten and Bratislava Stare Mesto. Wilten is our central Innsbruck store for practical dispatch purposes. Stare Mesto is the old-town Bratislava branch.

The newest 2025 additions are Brno Veveri and Ljubljana Center. Veveri is our central Brno branch. Ljubljana Center is the downtown Ljubljana shop.

When several stores share a city, use the city nuance above before checking inventory. Inventory itself remains in the SQL projection, keyed by the store IDs from the JSON files in this directory.

Store JSON records include generated branch coordinates for payment-risk and distance checks.

# Returns

Generated return workflow records live here. Return records only exist for paid checked-out baskets.

Return statuses are compact: `requested`, `approved`, `rejected`, `refund_pending`, `replacement_pending`, and `closed`.

Use `payment_id`, `basket_id`, and `customer_id` to reconcile a return with the linked payment and checked-out basket.

# Baskets

Generated customer baskets live here. Basket files list customer, store, lifecycle status, and requested line items.

Basket records include `created_at`, generated from world time, so tasks can refer to basket recency such as "the last basket".

Baskets do not carry availability flags; compare basket lines with store inventory when checkout eligibility matters.

Discounted baskets carry a `discount` object with `percent`, `reason_code`, and `issuer_id`.

Basket status is lifecycle state. `active` baskets may still need inventory checks before checkout; `checked_out` baskets are historical orders and may have linked records under `/proc/payments/` and `/proc/returns/`.

# Payments

Generated payment records live here. Current payment files represent one payment attempt for a `checked_out` basket.

Older payments may have `basket_archived: true`. Those basket records have aged out of `/proc/baskets/` into cold storage, so the payment file carries its own `lines` snapshot with SKU, quantity, and unit price.

Payment statuses are compact:

- `paid`: payment completed successfully.
- `requires_3ds_action`: payment is stuck in the simulated 3DS workflow.

Stuck payments include a `three_ds` object with legacy challenge status, failure reason, attempts, and maximum attempts. The `three_ds.status` values intentionally use inherited 3DS codes; consult the payment policy before deciding whether a payment can be recovered.

Payment fingerprints are opaque instrument/device identifiers. `observed_lat` and `observed_lon` are checkout-time coordinates from the payment risk log, not a customer address.

WITH targets AS (
  SELECT 'HND-29P0IVOV' AS sku, '/proc/catalog/hand_tools/pliers_wrenches/HND-29P0IVOV.json' AS path
  UNION ALL SELECT 'PWR-20JT29HY','/proc/catalog/power_tools/cordless_saw_sander/PWR-20JT29HY.json'
  UNION ALL SELECT 'SFE-27AU0QYP','/proc/catalog/safety_gear/safety_eyewear/SFE-27AU0QYP.json'
  UNION ALL SELECT 'PLB-2SJWSPPD','/proc/catalog/plumbing/valves_connectors/PLB-2SJWSPPD.json'
  UNION ALL SELECT 'SFE-6JP1X7OX','/proc/catalog/safety_gear/work_gloves/SFE-6JP1X7OX.json'
  UNION ALL SELECT 'STO-1F1H9FYY','/proc/catalog/storage/tool_boxes_bags/STO-1F1H9FYY.json'
)
SELECT t.sku,t.path,i.available_today,
       CASE WHEN i.available_today>=4 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS qualifies
FROM targets t
LEFT JOIN inventory i ON i.sku=t.sku AND i.store_id='store_graz_jakomini'
ORDER BY t.sku;

WITH target_store AS (
  SELECT id, name FROM stores WHERE name LIKE '%Bratislava%Stare Mesto%'
), targets(label, brand, series_like, model, pname, a1k, a1v, a2k, a2v, a3k, a3v) AS (
  VALUES
  ('wago_wiring_device','Wago','%Wago 221 222%','DLX-KMG','Wiring Device','device_type','terminal clamp','color_family','White','ip_rating','IP20'),
  ('3m_safety_eyewear','3M','%3M SecureFit SF%','6OX-K48','Safety Eyewear','lens_color','Gray','size','S',NULL,NULL),
  ('giacomini_valve_connector','Giacomini','%Giacomini Universal%','1SW-PKA','Valve and Connector','connector_type','ball valve','diameter','16 mm','thread_type','none'),
  ('metabo_workshop_dgs','Metabo','%Metabo Workshop KGS%','33X-O4H','Workshop Drill Grinder and Sander','machine_type','drill press','voltage','400 V','power','2200 W'),
  ('stihl_lawn_mower','Stihl','%Stihl AP System MS%','3ID-OYQ','Lawn Mower','power_source','petrol','cutting_width','32 cm',NULL,NULL),
  ('bd_angle_grinder','Black+Decker','%Black+Decker Heavy Duty BD%','2ZM-SG1','Corded Angle Grinder','disc_diameter','115 mm','power','2200 W',NULL,NULL)
), matched AS (
  SELECT t.label, p.sku, p.path
  FROM targets t
  JOIN products p
    ON p.brand = t.brand
   AND p.series LIKE t.series_like
   AND p.model = t.model
   AND p.name = t.pname
   AND json_extract(p.properties, '$.' || t.a1k) = t.a1v
   AND json_extract(p.properties, '$.' || t.a2k) = t.a2v
   AND (t.a3k IS NULL OR json_extract(p.properties, '$.' || t.a3k) = t.a3v)
)
SELECT m.label, m.sku, m.path, s.id as store_id, i.available_today
FROM matched m
CROSS JOIN target_store s
LEFT JOIN inventory i ON i.sku = m.sku AND i.store_id = s.id
ORDER BY m.label;
SELECT name, sql FROM sqlite_schema WHERE type='table' AND name IN ('products','stores','inventory');

)"
